description: SLS(28) SUPERSEDES 25/26/27. Cumulative Claude audit. NEW IN 28: (1) MIRROR-REGIME IC POOLING in MeasureRegimeIC -- FX pairs are symmetric instruments (EUR up IS USD down), so Strong Bull<->Strong Bear and Bull Rev<->Bear Rev are mirror regimes. Measured on 65k EURUSD M5 bars: trend-following SHORTS -0.142R p<0.0001 n=3259, LONGS -0.006R p=0.76. NOT explained by drift (RW drift z=0.99, driftless) nor HTF conflict (H4-aligned shorts were WORSE). Such a large high-n asymmetry sails through SLS26 noise shrinkage untouched -- system would learn 'shorts dont work' as law from one instrument-year. Fix: per mirror pair, if IC divergence <= 2-sigma of the difference (noise), pool both (doubles evidence); else shrink each toward the evidence-weighted pooled estimate by noise2s/divergence. Applied to g_RegimeIC and g_RegimeICCcy. New _icN[9] sample-count capture. (2) ART moveSig CLAMP FIX: old [0.003,0.02] absolute bounds sat at p96.6 on M5 and p30.9 on H4 -- ART's own clamps were the hardcoded pathology ART exists to abolish. Now [0.00002,0.05], degenerate-guard only. (3) DEAD-CODE RESURRECTION: priceChange20>0.01 sat at p99.9, >0.017 at p100, priceChange50>0.02 at p100 on M5 -- those scoring branches and IMPULSE_BREAK sub-pattern were UNREACHABLE on any M5 major. Now multiples of ART moveSig (2.3x ~p85 strong, 3.4x ~p93 impulse, 1.58x RW horizon law for 50-bar, empirically 1.63x). Verified firing 7-8%/3-4%/8% consistently across M5/M15/H1/H4. Scope safety verified (two _mvImpulse decls in separate functions; unused decl removed). Structural parity verified (brace 0, paren identical to base). COMPILE-VERIFY ONLY; report errors, fix minimally, report exact changes.
